title: Traces (EADF2026)
synopsis:
In war-torn Ukraine, sexual violence and torture are systematically weaponized to shatter lives. Refusing to remain silent, former captive turned activist Iryna Dovhan journeys through newly de-occupied territories to document the harrowing accounts of fellow survivors. Directed by a wartime survivor herself, this powerful documentary weaves a portrait of collective trauma, capturing not just a record of war crimes, but a fierce, unified crusade of women transforming deep personal pain into transformative justice. Asian Premiere.

 

Panorama Audience Award, Berlin International Film Festival
CPH:DOX
Sydney Film Festival

synopsis_lang.en:
In war-torn Ukraine, sexual violence and torture are systematically weaponized to shatter lives. Refusing to remain silent, former captive turned activist Iryna Dovhan journeys through newly de-occupied territories to document the harrowing accounts of fellow survivors. Directed by a wartime survivor herself, this powerful documentary weaves a portrait of collective trauma, capturing not just a record of war crimes, but a fierce, unified crusade of women transforming deep personal pain into transformative justice. Asian Premiere.

 

Panorama Audience Award, Berlin International Film Festival
CPH:DOX
Sydney Film Festival
synopsis_lang.zh_hk:
「花會繼續開，草會一直長。所以，我也可以。」在飽受戰火摧殘的烏克蘭東部，性侵與囚禁成了最齷齪的戰爭武器。曾遭幽禁的倖存者拒絕失聲，毅然走進剛收復的國土，收集並記錄受害女性的血淚證詞。導演同樣曾遭俄軍性暴力對待，以紀錄片揭示戰爭暴行留下的身心烙印，打破社會長久以來的禁忌與沉默，將痛苦轉化為力量，展現出倖存者的堅韌和勇氣。她們並肩築起療癒的姊妹情誼，將私密的個體創傷化為爭取公義的武器。亞洲首映。

 

柏林影展 全景單元觀眾票選獎
哥本哈根國際紀錄片電影節
悉尼電影節
